subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF, World Bank, OECD, ADB, WorldRiskIndex, RSF, CSIS-AMTI) + Philippine official (BSP, PSA) + independent press (Rappler, Inquirer, BusinessWorld).
Source-lean: HIGH Anglophone (English is an official working language); offset by genuinely independent local outlets (note Rappler/Ressa persecution history).
A genuine demographic dividend offsets governance and extreme climate-disaster exposure; the South China Sea is an acute geopolitical tail.

economic.notes: Fast regional growth on services/remittances/electronics and a credible central bank, against deteriorating fiscal space.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt rising to ~60% GDP with a ~5.3% deficit; the IMF flags 'vanishing fiscal space,' worsened by a flood-control corruption scandal. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: Bangko Sentral is a credible inflation-targeter with real autonomy, moving data-driven on shocks. (BSP MPR 2026)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The peso is not a reserve currency; neutral. (BSP)

inflation.notes: Was low (~1.7%) but a Middle East oil shock pushed it above band (~4%+), expected to re-anchor long-term. (BSP)

banking_stability.notes: Sound and remittance-supported (CAR ~16%); the BSP flags credit-concentration and cyber risk. (BSP; OECD 2026)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Business-process outsourcing (~8% GDP), record remittances, and electronics; no resource curse. Near-term drag from a 19% US tariff and the AI threat to BPO. (BSP; OECD)

institutional.notes: Weak rule of law and patronage-driven administration, with usable statistics and a press improved but still pressured.

rule_of_law.notes: A massive flood-control corruption scandal reaching the palace; weak courts, dynastic capture, and impunity. (Al Jazeera; Bloomberg)

statistical_integrity.notes: PSA/BSP data is credible and usable, with minor caveats. (IMF SDDS)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Patronage-driven, with 1,800+ unfinished health facilities and procurement rot exposed by the flood scandal. (Inquirer)

press_freedom.notes: Friendlier than the prior administration (RSF 114/180, up from 134) but still 'very serious': red-tagging persists and the Ressa cases linger. (RSF)

political_social.notes: Acute dynastic political conflict and a weak-rights legacy, against an open residency route and a genuine demographic dividend.

political_stability.notes: An acute Marcos-Duterte feud, a second VP impeachment heading to a Senate trial, and the largest protests since 2013. (TIME; SCMP)

civil_liberties.notes: An extrajudicial-killing legacy (the drug war, now at the ICC), improving under the current administration but with weak enforcement. (Al Jazeera)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: The SRRV retiree visa was restructured in 2025 (age lowered, streamlined, indefinite stay), favorable to US citizens; English widely spoken. Open, lawful, durable. (PRA)

social_cohesion.notes: A Mindanao insurgency legacy and many private armed groups, but the BARMM peace process is advancing; fragile. (ICG)

demographics.notes: A young population and a genuine demographic dividend - a real structural positive. (World Bank/OECD)

geopolitical.notes: A deepening US alliance with an acute, ongoing South China Sea flashpoint.

alliance_reliability.notes: A US treaty ally (MDT/EDCA), deepening materially. (CSIS)

conflict_involvement.notes: Acute, ongoing South China Sea clashes with China (water-cannon/ramming at Scarborough and Second Thomas Shoal) - a live flashpoint. (CSIS-AMTI)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open-ish, no sanctions, relatively free capital flows. (OECD)

physical_practical.notes: Among the most disaster-exposed countries on earth, with uneven healthcare and infrastructure.

climate.notes: Among the most disaster-exposed on Earth - frequent intense typhoons, storm surge, sea-level, moderate-low readiness. (Germanwatch CRI; WB CCDR)

healthcare.notes: Out-of-pocket spending ~45%; PhilHealth strained and uneven, though a medical-tourism tier exists. (World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Improving ('Build Better More') but congested and weak, with flood-control corruption undermining delivery. (OECD; CNN)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate, with strong regional variation. (regional reporting)

disaster_insurance.notes: A ~98% catastrophe protection gap against extreme exposure; very low penetration. (ADB)

summary: Fast regional growth on services, remittances, and electronics, a credible central bank, a genuine demographic dividend, and an open, English-friendly retiree-residency route. The drags are weak rule of law (a palace-level corruption scandal), acute dynastic political conflict, an extrajudicial-killing legacy, deteriorating fiscal space, and among the world's most extreme climate-disaster exposure. The South China Sea is an acute geopolitical flashpoint.
